mappings.rationale: Directly covers magnetic phenomena, magnetic materials, ferromagnetism, paramagnetism, diamagnetism, and atomic structure and magnetism central to this section.
Directly relevant to the electromagnetic theory of light, Maxwell's equations, the electromagnetic spectrum, and wave-particle duality of photons discussed in this section.
Covers electricity, magnetism, electromagnetic radiation, and the fundamental principles of electrostatics and electrodynamics in this section.
Directly relevant to Faraday's law of electromagnetic induction, field theory, and the historical development of electromagnetism in this section.
Relevant to electrical conduction in metals, thermoelectric effects, and the electromagnetic properties of superconductors.
Covers electrical conduction in metals and semiconductors, magnetic phenomena in solids, and the electronic structure underlying electrical properties.
Covers the quantum theory of radiation, photoelectric effect, Compton effect, and the wave-particle duality of photons discussed in this section.
Directly relevant to relativistic electrodynamics and the four-dimensional formulation of electromagnetic theory discussed in this section.
Covers the historical development of concepts concerning electricity, magnetism, and the nature of light from classical to modern theories.
Relevant to early theories of light and the development of concepts concerning the nature of light discussed in this section.
Covers generation and transmission of ac power, electromagnetic induction, and practical applications of electricity discussed in this section.
Relevant to semiconductor devices, electron emission phenomena, and the electrical properties of materials at the nanoscale.
Covers the interaction of electromagnetic fields with matter and the fundamental electrical and magnetic properties of materials.
Covers the philosophical foundations of field theory, electromagnetic theory, and the nature of electromagnetic radiation.
Relevant to the quantum theory of radiation, atomic absorption and emission processes, and the interaction of electromagnetic radiation with matter.
Relevant to piezoelectricity, dielectric properties of crystals, and the interaction of electromagnetic radiation with crystalline materials.
Relevant to magnetic properties of materials, magnetic minerals, and the classification of magnetic substances discussed in this section.
Relevant to the mathematical formulation of electromagnetic theory including Maxwell's equations, field equations, and wave equations.
Relevant to symmetry principles underlying Maxwell's equations and the gauge symmetries of electromagnetic field theory.
Relevant to the historical development of concepts concerning the nature of light and the scientific revolution that influenced electromagnetic theory.
Provides historical context for the development of theories concerning electricity, magnetism, and the nature of light.
Relevant to terrestrial magnetism and the Earth's magnetic field discussed in this section's coverage of magnetism.
Relevant to electromagnetic induction, photovoltaic effects, and the practical applications of electricity generation.
